channel.title: Blue Cross VT News Mentions
channel.description: Mentions of BCBSVT, Blue Cross VT, and Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont from Vermont news outlets.

item.title: Vermont Business Magazine: Vermont Women’s Fund awards over $400,000 in grants to local nonprofits
item.description:
Source: Vermont Business Magazine

Matched terms: Mental health

The Vermont Women’s Fund has awarded over $400,000 in grants to various local nonprofits, including mental health service providers.

Why included: Supports community health initiatives relevant to BCBSVT's mission.

Read the original story

item.title: Vermont Business Magazine: Burlington encourages residents to prepare for hot weather
item.description:
Source: Vermont Business Magazine

Matched terms: Vermont health agencies, Mental health

Burlington officials are advising residents to prepare for hot weather, noting that vulnerable populations face higher risks of heat-related illness.

Why included: Public health advisory regarding vulnerable populations.

Read the original story

item.title: VTDigger: Vermont’s largest health insurer wants to offer a cheaper plan. Can it actually help Vermonters?
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: VTDigger

Matched terms: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont, Vermont's largest health insurer

BCBSVT is seeking to offer more affordable health insurance plans to address declining enrollment in the state's marketplace.

Why included: Directly concerns BCBSVT strategy and public affordability.

Read the original story

item.title: Valley News: Randolph hospital offers extended-release opioid treatment in emergency department
item.description:
Source: Valley News

Matched terms: Hospitals, Opioids & addiction

Randolph hospital has introduced extended-release opioid treatment within its emergency department.

Why included: Tracks regional hospital service expansions for addiction treatment.

Read the original story

item.title: WCAX: More cuts could be coming at UVM Health amid statewide cost-cutting push
item.description:
Source: WCAX

Matched terms: Blue Cross

UVM Health is considering further service cuts as part of a statewide effort to lower hospital costs and insurance premiums. The report highlights that BCBSVT premiums have risen significantly over the last five years.

Why included: Names BCBSVT and discusses premium drivers.

Read the original story

item.title: VTDigger: Vermont Conversation: Are smartphones birth control? Economist Caitlin Myers on sex, abortion access and talking across divides
item.description:
Source: VTDigger

Matched terms: Reproductive health

An economist discusses how smartphone usage may be impacting sexual behavior and reproductive health trends.

Why included: Contextualizes broader public health and reproductive health trends.

Read the original story

item.title: WCAX: New York to end Essential Plan healthcare coverage for 450,000 residents
item.description:
Source: WCAX

Matched terms: Health insurance, Health care

New York state is ending its Essential Plan healthcare coverage for 450,000 residents this June.

Why included: Regional health coverage policy changes.

Read the original story

item.title: WCAX: 2 charged in connection with fatal overdose in Stowe
item.description:
Source: WCAX

Matched terms: Opioids & addiction

Two individuals have been charged following a fatal drug overdose in Stowe.

Why included: Monitors local public health and addiction issues.

Read the original story

item.title: VTDigger: UVM Health cuts 142 jobs — an estimated $9.5 million in staff positions
item.description:
Source: VTDigger

Matched terms: UVM Health, Hospitals

UVM Health is cutting 142 staff positions to address a projected $300 million budget deficit.

Why included: Hospital financial instability impacts health care delivery.

Read the original story

item.title: VTDigger: The state can’t fix Vermont’s hospitals by telling them what to do
item.description:
Source: VTDigger

Matched terms: Hospitals

An opinion piece argues that Vermont hospitals require price reform rather than state-mandated operational directives to achieve transformation.

Why included: Discusses hospital cost regulation and policy.

Read the original story

item.title: VTDigger: Waiting for prior authorization almost killed me
item.description:
Source: VTDigger

Matched terms: Prior authorization & claims

A patient shares a personal account of how delays in the prior authorization process negatively impacted their health.

Why included: Public sentiment regarding prior authorization processes.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Blue Cross VT recognized for customer service excellence - Vermont Business Magazine
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross VT, Blue Cross, Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont, bluecrossvt.org

BCBSVT’s customer service call center was recognized as a top performer for small- to mid-sized organizations by the Service Quality Measurement Group.

Why included: Positive recognition for BCBSVT operations.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont seeks smaller premium increase this year - WPTZ
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont, Blue Cross, Blue Cross VT

BCBSVT is requesting single-digit premium increases, which would mark the smallest hike in several years.

Why included: Directly relates to BCBSVT premium filings.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Blue Cross VT Mountain Days May 9 - Vermont Business Magazine
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross VT, Blue Cross, bluecrossvt.org

Blue Cross VT is hosting its annual Mountain Days event at Mt. Wantastiquet on May 9. The event encourages Vermonters to engage in physical activity and community connection.

Why included: Highlights BCBSVT community engagement and brand presence.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Blue Cross of Vermont Rebounds After Near Collapse - Seven Days Vermont
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross of Vermont, Blue Cross, Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont

Blue Cross Blue Shield of Vermont reported over $50 million in operating gains for 2025. This marks a significant financial recovery following previous years of losses.

Why included: Reports on BCBSVT financial health and stability.

Read the original story

item.title: Brattleboro Reformer: Balint co-sponsors the Mental Health Career Promotion Act
item.description:
Source: Brattleboro Reformer

Matched terms: Health care, Mental health

U.S. Representative Becca Balint has co-sponsored the Mental Health Career Promotion Act. The legislation aims to strengthen the behavioral and mental health care workforce.

Why included: Legislative action on health care workforce development.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Beth Roberts assumes role as CEO of Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont - Vermont Business Magazine
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont, Blue Cross, Blue Cross VT, bluecrossvt.org

Beth Roberts has officially assumed the role of president and CEO of Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont. She succeeds outgoing CEO Don George following a transition period.

Why included: Names new BCBSVT executive leadership.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Battle of the Giants: Understanding the Public Split Between Blue Cross and UVM Health Network - Compass Vermont
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross, BCBSVT, Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont

The relationship between BCBSVT and the UVM Health Network has shifted from private negotiation to public confrontation. This conflict impacts the broader Vermont health care landscape.

Why included: Direct impact on BCBSVT provider relations.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Vermont’s largest health insurer tells you to avoid the state’s largest hospital in new marketing campaign - VTDigger
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Vermont's largest health insurer, BCBSVT, Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont, Health insurance, Hospitals

In a new public awareness campaign, BlueCross BlueShield of Vermont has joined four independent Chittenden County healthcare facilities in urging clients to seek less expensive care outside of Vermont’s large academic medical centers. The newly launched website, VT Affordable Care, offers a simp ...

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: These Vermonters are about to lose their Medicare Advantage plans and they're scrambling - Burlington Free Press
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont, Vermont Blue Advantage, Blue Cross

Vermonters enrolled in Vermont Blue Advantage Medicare plans are facing uncertainty as they prepare to lose their coverage. Many are struggling to find alternatives for their medical needs.

Why included: Impacts BCBSVT members and public perception.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Vermont Blue Advantage will not sell plans at all in 2026 - VTDigger
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Vermont Blue Advantage, Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont, Blue Cross

Blue Cross Blue Shield of Vermont announced that Vermont Blue Advantage will not offer any Medicare Advantage plans in 2026. This decision affects 26,000 individual plan members.

Why included: Major change to BCBSVT product offerings.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: These are the health insurers that quit Medicare Advantage - Modern Healthcare
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Medicare Advantage, Medicare, Health insurance, Health care

Modern Healthcare reports on various health insurers that are exiting the Medicare Advantage market. The article tracks national trends in the insurance industry.

Why included: Provides national context for BCBSVT business decisions.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Blue Cross Blue Shield to drop Medicare Advantage plans by year’s end - WCAX
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross, Vermont Blue Advantage

Vermont Blue Advantage is discontinuing its Medicare Advantage plans due to significant financial losses. The insurer reported losing over $62 million last year.

Why included: Explains financial drivers behind BCBSVT product changes.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Vermont Blue Advantage to end Medicare plans - MyChamplainValley.com
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Vermont Blue Advantage

Vermont Blue Advantage has confirmed it will end its Medicare plans. The announcement marks the conclusion of these specific offerings in the state.

Why included: Significant news regarding BCBSVT product portfolio.

Read the original story

Article body fetch warning: HTTP 403 while fetching https://www.mychamplainvalley.com/news/vermont-blue-advantage-to-end-medicare-plans/

item.title: Google News Search: Tens of thousands Vermonters are set to lose Medicare Advantage option in 2026 - VTDigger
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont, Vermont Blue Advantage, Blue Cross

CMS data indicates that Vermont Blue Advantage will discontinue its individual Medicare Advantage plans for the 2026 coverage year. This change affects a significant portion of the 51,600 Vermonters currently enrolled in such plans.

Why included: Names Vermont Blue Advantage and impacts a large member base.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Vermont Blue Advantage to end Medicare Advantage plans this year - WPTZ
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Vermont Blue Advantage

Vermont Blue Advantage has announced it will terminate all of its Medicare Advantage plans at the end of the current calendar year.

Why included: Directly impacts BCBSVT product offerings and member coverage.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Blue Cross VT names Beth-Ann Roberts as next president and CEO - Vermont Business Magazine
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross VT, Blue Cross, Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont

Beth-Ann Roberts has been appointed as the next president and CEO of Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont, effective January 10, 2026. She will succeed retiring CEO Don George following a two-month transition period.

Why included: Major corporate leadership announcement for BCBSVT.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Blue Cross Vermont names Beth-Ann Roberts as president and CEO - Vermont Business Magazine
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross VT, Blue Cross, Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont, bluecrossvt.org

The Board of Directors for Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont has selected Beth-Ann Roberts to serve as the company's next president and CEO.

Why included: Major corporate leadership announcement for BCBSVT.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Blue Cross Blue Shield and MVP Health rate increases approved - MyChamplainValley.com
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross

Regulators have officially approved rate increases for health insurance plans offered by Blue Cross Blue Shield and MVP Health.

Why included: Regulatory approval of BCBSVT premium rate increases.

Read the original story

Article body fetch warning: HTTP 403 while fetching https://www.mychamplainvalley.com/news/local-news/vermont/blue-cross-blue-shield-and-mvp-health-rate-increases-approved/

item.title: Bennington Banner: Green Mountain Camp launches the Sarah Bradley and Edith Bradley Memorial Endowment
item.description:
Source: Bennington Banner

Matched terms: Public health

The Green Mountain Camp has established a memorial endowment inspired by the founders' historical focus on addressing the needs of underserved rural children.

Why included: Mentions public health history, but not directly relevant to BCBSVT.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Vermont regulators approve a trimmed increase to 2026 health insurance premiums for large employers - VTDigger
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont, Blue Cross

The Green Mountain Care Board has approved a 13.7% average premium increase for large employer health insurance plans offered by Blue Cross Blue Shield of Vermont for 2026.

Why included: Regulatory action on BCBSVT premium rates.

Read the original story

item.title: Bennington Banner: Sanders calls for investigation into vaccine committee firings
item.description:
Source: Bennington Banner

Matched terms: Vaccines

Senator Bernie Sanders is calling for an investigation into the dismissal of all members of the CDC's Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices by the Secretary of Health and Human Services.

Why included: Public health policy news involving federal vaccine oversight.

Read the original story

item.title: VTDigger Facebook: VTDigger Facebook post: BlueCross BlueShield wants to offer a new suite of cheaper plans, but the state’
item.description:
Source: VTDigger Facebook

Matched terms: Blue Cross, Health insurance, Health care

VTDigger's Post VTDigger 2h · BlueCross BlueShield wants to offer a new suite of cheaper plans, but the state’s primary healthcare regulator is casting doubt on whether that would actually yield any real savings.vtdigger.orgVermont’s largest health insurer wants to offer a cheaper plan. Can it actually help Vermonters? - VTDiggerAll reactions:814 comments2 sharesLikeCommentMost ...

Comments:

  • Janet Fitzpatrick: Allow people over 30 to buy Blue Cross Blue Shield catastrophic plans! Those plans are much more affordable.
  • Mark Byland: sp/healthcare/healthprofit
  • Kristin Bryn Reid: The health insurance system in Vermont is voing exactly as designed for the last 20 or so years We tried to warn you
  • Dylan Greer: Or hear me out… we pay a boat load of money to insurance companies… they can pay for our healthcare
  • Janice Wright: Cheaper plan = lower monthly payments, but higher deductibles, higher co-pays, less coverage, and fewer in-network doctors
  • Jay Files: My work just switched to MVP gold great insurance we had blue cross it covers almost nothing worthless insurance you have to fight with them for everything
  • Amy Beth: "higher" deductible plan my deductible is already $9500 so unless they are significantly dropping the monthly premium which is between $809-900 a month for one person this isn't helpful. Health insurance for those of us who have to purchase it from the marketplace is ridiculous.
  • Ruth Miller: I dunno...I just got a class action suit settlement payment from BCBS. I'm done with them, especially after leaving me with no gap coverage after they pulled Blue Advantage out of VT. They seem to lose money on a continuing basis.
  • Matthew Funk: It’s almost like y’all already Fcked it up

Read the original story
